entities.name: Crow Indian Reservation

entities.name: Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
Western Union
entities.context: Telegraph company that sent messages about S. 716

entities.name: George Redfield

events.description: S. 716 was introduced by Senator James E. Murray to authorize the issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters

events.location: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
events.description: The committee considered S. 716 and recommended amendments
S. 716 passed the Senate, authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to sell George Peters' land to a Crow Indian under existing regulations
The committee considered S. 716 and reported it favorably without amendment
S. 716 was cleared for the President's signature

financial_transactions.description: Sale of George Peters' land to a Crow Indian under existing regulations

relationships.subject: James E. Murray
relationships.context: Murray introduced S. 716 to authorize the issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters

relationships.subject: George Peters
relationships.context: Peters indicated his desire to sell his land to Redfield, another Crow Indian, in 1921

relationships.subject: Joseph C. O'Mahoney
relationships.context: O'Mahoney was the chairman of the Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs that considered S. 716

relationships.subject: Oscar L. Chapman
relationships.context: Chapman, as Undersecretary of the Interior, recommended amendments to S. 716 to permit the sale of George Peters' land under existing regulations

correspondence.recipient_title: Chairman, Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
correspondence.subject: Report on S. 716 authorizing patent in fee to George Peters, Crow Indian
correspondence.action_requested: Recommendation to enact bill if amended to allow sale to a Crow Indian under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee
correspondence.outcome: Report forwarded to Senator Murray on March 10; bill subsequently amended as suggested

correspondence.subject: Introduction of S. 716 to authorize patent in fee
correspondence.outcome: Peters was informed of the bill's introduction and expressed his desire for its passage

correspondence.sender_title: Chairman, Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
correspondence.subject: Report on S. 716 and recommended amendments
correspondence.action_requested: Consideration of the committee's report and recommended amendments
correspondence.outcome: Murray was informed of the committee's report and the bill was subsequently amended

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Bill was introduced and referred to committee

legislative_actions.committee: Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
legislative_actions.outcome: Committee reported the bill with recommended amendments
Bill was cleared for the President's signature
